summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened his message with a lighthearted illustration about stirring coffee, using it as a metaphor for how God stirs our lives. He preached from Matthew 21:1-11, focusing on how Jesus's triumphal entry into Jerusalem 'stirred the whole city.' Pastor Travis explained that this event, which fulfilled ancient prophecy, was a declaration of Jesus as the Messiah and a symbol of peace and humility. He connected this biblical stirring to the spiritual stirrings God initiates in our lives today, emphasizing that such divine agitation, while often uncomfortable, is purposeful. God stirs us to push us out of complacency, to grow us into maturity, and to propel us toward the mission He has for us. The sermon highlighted that a stirring is a sign of God's work, whether it's in an individual heart, a family, or a nation, and it often precedes healing, purpose, and salvation. Pastor Travis passionately called the congregation to become 'stir crazy'—restless and impatient with spiritual stagnation, societal brokenness, and the lostness of those around them. He concluded with a powerful personal testimony of his own salvation and a rallying cry for Impact Church to actively bring people to the hope found in Jesus, especially as they prepared for Resurrection Sunday.
mainPoints: God intentionally stirs our lives to move us out of comfort and into our divine purpose and mission.
Jesus's entry into Jerusalem stirred the city because He is the promised Messiah, bringing salvation, peace, and humility.
We are called to be 'stir crazy' for the lost, actively sharing the hope of salvation and bringing people to Christ.

versesMentioned.text: As they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples, saying to them, 'Go to the village ahead of you, and at once you will find a donkey tied there, with her colt by her. Untie them and bring them to me. If anyone says anything to you, say that the Lord needs them, and he will send them right away.' This took place to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet: 'Say to Daughter Zion, "See, your king comes to you, gentle and riding on a donkey, and on a colt, the foal of a donkey."' The disciples went and did as Jesus had instructed them. They brought the donkey and the colt and placed their cloaks on them for Jesus to sit on. A very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road, while others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road. The crowds that went ahead of him and those that followed shouted, 'Hosanna to the Son of David!' 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!' 'Hosanna in the highest heaven!' When Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred and asked, 'Who is this?' The crowds answered, 'This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee.'
Rejoice greatly, Daughter Zion! Shout, Daughter Jerusalem! See, your king comes to you, righteous and victorious, lowly and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.
And being found in appearance as a man, he humbled himself by becoming obedient to death— even death on a cross!
For an angel went down at a certain time into the pool and stirred up the water; then whoever stepped in first after the stirring of the water was made well of whatever disease he had.
The woman gave birth to a boy and named him Samson. He grew and the Lord blessed him, and the Spirit of the Lord began to stir him while he was in Mahaneh Dan, between Zorah and Eshtaol.
Like an eagle that stirs up its nest and hovers over its young, that spreads its wings to catch them and carries them aloft.
For I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes: first to the Jew, then to the Gentile.
How, then, can they call on the one they have not believed in? And how can they believe in the one of whom they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone preaching to them?
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Matthew 21:10 - 'When Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred...'
Monday.action: Identify one area of your life where you feel God might be stirring you out of comfort. Write it down.
Monday.prayer: God, open my eyes to see where You are at work in my life. Help me to welcome Your stirring, even when it's uncomfortable.

Tuesday.scripture: Deuteronomy 32:11 - 'Like an eagle that stirs up its nest and hovers over its young...'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a past challenge. Acknowledge one way it helped you grow or 'learn to fly.'
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your faithful care. Help me to trust that You are hovering over me, even in seasons that feel like a freefall.

Wednesday.scripture: Romans 10:9 - 'If you declare with your mouth, "Jesus is Lord," and believe in your heart... you will be saved.'
Wednesday.action: Take a moment to personally thank God for the gift of salvation. If you've never done so, consider praying to confess Jesus as Lord.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for paying the ultimate price for my salvation. I believe You are Lord and that God raised You from the dead.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 1:16 - 'For I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation...'
Thursday.action: Pray for one person in your life who needs to hear the hope of the gospel. Send them a simple, encouraging text message.
Thursday.prayer: God, give me courage and remove any shame. Fill me with Your power and love for [Name] as I seek to share Your hope.

Friday.scripture: John 5:4 - '...an angel went down... and stirred up the water; then whoever stepped in first... was made well...'
Friday.action: Is there an area where you need God's healing—spiritual, emotional, or relational? Take one small step of faith toward wholeness today (e.g., seeking prayer, starting a conversation).
Friday.prayer: Healer God, You stir the waters for my restoration. I choose to step in by faith today, trusting You for my wholeness.

Saturday.scripture: Zechariah 9:9 - 'See, your king comes to you, righteous and victorious, lowly and riding on a donkey...'
Saturday.action: Prepare your heart for Resurrection Sunday. Invite at least one person to join you for a church service or watch online.
Saturday.prayer: King Jesus, I welcome You into my heart and my city. Stir a fresh hunger for You in me and use me to bring others into Your presence tomorrow.
